---
title: "Counselling"
transition_status: "updated"
transition_comment: "update"
---

This page provides an overview of counselling, advisory services, and financial support resources available to students at Frankfurt School.

Frankfurt School follows a [“We Care” support concept](https://www.frankfurt-school.de/en/students-alumni), with dedicated study advisors and specialised support services for questions related to studies, examinations, programme organisation, financial matters, health, housing, and personal challenges.

::: callout-note
Scholarships are listed separately on the [scholarships](scholarships_and_awards.qmd) page.
This page focuses on counselling, advisory services, and non-scholarship financial support.
:::

## First point of contact

* **Study Advisory**
  Each student has a dedicated study advisor who serves as the first point of contact for questions related to the programme, modules, examinations, invoices, feedback, or personal challenges.
  [Student support at Frankfurt School](https://www.frankfurt-school.de/en/students-alumni)

## Counselling and personal support

* **Life & Health Counseling**
  Support for students navigating personal challenges, including psychosocial counselling and coaching.
  [Life & Health Counseling](https://www.frankfurt-school.de/en/students-alumni/life-and-health-counseling)

* **Disability-related support**
  The Disability Service Office supports students with disabilities and chronic diseases.
  [Life & Health Counseling](https://www.frankfurt-school.de/en/students-alumni/life-and-health-counseling)

* **International Office**
  Support for incoming exchange students and Frankfurt School students going abroad, including advice on settling in Germany and studying at partner universities.
  [International Office](https://www.frankfurt-school.de/en/students-alumni/international-office)

## Financial support

* **Student Financing overview**
  Overview of financing options, including scholarships, loans, funds, BAföG, U.S. financial aid, and country-specific funding.
  [Student Financing](https://www.frankfurt-school.de/en/students-alumni/financing)

* **Loans and funds**
  Information on Brain Capital Income Share Agreements, KfW Student Loan, and other loan-based financing options.
  [Loans and Funds](https://www.frankfurt-school.de/en/students-alumni/financing/loans-and-funds)

* **BAföG and German government funding**
  Information on BAföG, Auslands-BAföG, and education loans from the Federal Office of Administration.
  [BAföG and German Government Funding](https://www.frankfurt-school.de/en/students-alumni/financing/bafoeg)

* **U.S. Financial Aid**
  Information for eligible U.S. students on Federal Student Aid and Direct Loan options.
  [U.S. Financial Aid](https://www.frankfurt-school.de/en/students-alumni/financing/us-financial-page)

* **Country-specific funding**
  Information on funding opportunities available in students’ home countries.
  [Other Country-Specific Funding](https://www.frankfurt-school.de/en/students-alumni/financing/other-country-specific-funding)

## Career-related counselling

* **Career Services**
  Personalised career counselling, application support, workshops, interview preparation, and employer events.
  [Career Services](https://www.frankfurt-school.de/en/students-alumni/career-services)

* **Career paths and application support**
  Guidance on CVs, cover letters, interviews, job search, and professional positioning.
  [Career Paths](https://www.frankfurt-school.de/en/students-alumni/career-services/career-paths)


<!--
Bamberg:

        On this page, we collect resources for student counselling.

        We are committed to ensuring a safe, diverse, and supportive university environment by being prepared to assist students in navigating challenges they may face.
        Our instructors, teaching assistants and staff are sensitized to the various resources available, allowing us to respond promptly and effectively to student needs.
        Whether it's providing guidance on study organization, addressing mental health concerns, or offering support in cases of discrimination or harassment, we are dedicated to helping students access the help they need.
        Our goal is to ensure that every student feels supported and empowered throughout their academic journey.


        ## Reflect - Advise - Network

        See [recommendations](https://hbsp.harvard.edu/inspiring-minds/career-advice-for-students){target=_blank} and [worksheet](https://he-product-images.s3.amazonaws.com/docs/Career-Reflection-Worksheet-DePaul-HBI.pdf).

        ## Student counselling at the University

        ::: callout-note
        Overview of [Conflict Counselling for Students](https://www.uni-bamberg.de/en/administration-and-institutions/advisory-services/for-students/){target=_blank}.
        :::

        Student counselling at the University ([Website](https://www.uni-bamberg.de/studium/im-studium/beratung-fuer-studierende/){target=_blank}) covers

        - Study organization
        - Course of study
        - Mental health issues
        - Discrimination
        - (Sexual) harassment, stalking, bullying
        - Financial and social problems
        - Special life circumstances

        Please feel free to write to us if you are not sure who to contact.
        To do so, please use our [contact form](https://www.uni-bamberg.de/en/studies/currently-enrolled/conflict-advisory-services-for-students/form/){target=_blank} and remain anonymous if you wish.

        In addition, there is 

        - the Bamberg Nightline: [Website](https://bamberg.nightlines.eu/){target=_blank}
        - [STUVE FAQ](https://stuve-bamberg.de/faq/){target=_blank}

        ## Course of study

        See [BAULA](https://baula.minf.uni-bamberg.de/welcome){target=_blank}

-->
